channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Cloudflare is removing in-place DNS record type changes on June 30, 2026 — your Pulumi runs will start failing
item.description: Cloudflare announced on January 23, 2026 that changing the type of an existing DNS record via the API is deprecated. End-of-life: June 30, 2026. After that date, a PATCH or PUT against...

item.title: Architecture Teardown: How Replicate and Modal Host AI Models Using Kubernetes 1.34
item.description: Architecture Teardown: How Replicate and Modal Host AI Models Using Kubernetes 1.34 Replicate and Modal have emerged as two of the most popular platforms for hosting and serving AI models, each...

item.title: SwiftDeploy: Building a Self-Writing Infrastructure Manager with Policy Enforcement — A Complete Technical Walkthrough
item.description: How I built a CLI tool that generates its own infrastructure configs, manages a full containerised stack, enforces deployment policies through OPA, exposes Prometheus metrics, and produces a live...

item.title: I Didn’t Expect People to Care This Much About “Fancy Text”
item.description: It Started as a Tiny Side Tool Honestly, I thought this would be a “fun extra” tool. Not something people would seriously use. Because technically… 👉 It just changes text styles. That’s it. Or at...

item.title: Step-by-Step: Deploying a Multimodal AI Model with Llama 3.2 and FastAPI 0.112 on ECS 4.0
item.description: 68% of teams deploying multimodal AI models fail to hit production latency SLAs within 3 months of launch, wasting an average of $42k per failed initiative on idle GPU resources and engineer...

item.title: XMusic: A 10MB Cross-Platform Music Player Built with C++
item.description: In an era dominated by Electron and web technologies, we seem to have forgotten that desktop applications should be lighter and faster. Have you ever encountered these frustrations? ❌ Your simple...

item.title: What is a REST API: Four Words That Power the Internet
item.description: The Menu You Never Questioned You’ve probably walked into a restaurant hundreds of times without ever thinking about how it actually works. You sit down. A waiter appears. You point at something...

item.title: Stop Using Python for Your Gen AI Apps, Use Go and Genkit Instead
item.description: Introduction For the last few years, every Gen AI tutorial, framework, and "hello world" has assumed one thing: that you are writing Python. It made sense at the start. The research community...

item.title: Scaling Full-Stack Applications in High-Compliance Environments
item.description: Scaling Full-Stack Applications in High-Compliance Environments: Architectural Patterns and Lessons from Healthcare and Finance Systems Sai Kumar Jee GitHub: https://github.com/SaiKumarbomule...

item.title: Cold email in 2026: what indie hackers got wrong (with a Python validator I built to enforce the new rules)
item.description: If you've been sending B2B cold emails the same way for 18 months, your reply rate just dropped to ~0% and you don't know why. I rebuilt my outreach this week after my reply rate tanked from 4% to...

item.title: SAP bets $1.16B on 18-month-old German AI lab and says yes to NemoClaw
item.description: SAP plans to buy German AI startup Prior Labs and invest heavily in it. It is also prohibiting customers' agents use to a select few like Nvidia's NemoClaw.

item.title: ‘I Actually Thought He Was Going to Hit Me,’ OpenAI’s Greg Brockman Says of Elon Musk
item.description: OpenAI’s president wrapped his testimony on Tuesday by revealing a fiery meeting with Musk in 2017 and subsequent efforts to remove several board members.

item.title: Altara secures $7M to bridge the data gap that’s slowing down physical sciences
item.description: Altara’s AI aims to diagnose failures and help speed up R&D by unifying data siloed across spreadsheets and legacy systems.

item.title: Google Home’s Gemini AI can handle more complicated requests
item.description: Google Home users can now ask Gemini to complete more complex, multi-step tasks and combine multiple tasks in a single command. Google has updated Gemini for Home to Gemini 3.1, which it says will...

item.title: Apple agrees to pay iPhone owners $250 million for not delivering AI Siri
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $250 million to settle a class action lawsuit that accused it of misleading customers about the availability of its Apple Intelligence features. The proposed settlement...

item.title: Apple plans to make iOS 27 a Choose Your Own Adventure of AI models
item.description: With Apple's latest operating system updates, users will reportedly have their pick of which third-party AI models they want to use for a host of tasks.

item.title: ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet on his company’s monopoly: no one is coming for us
item.description: Christophe Fouquet, who became ASML's CEO in 2024 after more than a decade at the company, sat down with this editor on the rooftop deck of his Beverly Hills hotel Tuesday morning ahead of his...

item.title: Microsoft gives up on Xbox Copilot AI
item.description: Xbox is "winding down Copilot on mobile" and "will stop development of Copilot on console," new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma announced on Tuesday. The move follows Sharma's reorganization of the Xbox...

item.title: Widely used Daemon Tools disk app backdoored in monthlong supply-chain attack
item.description: Daemon Tools users: It's time to check your machines for stealthy infections, stat.

item.title: Apple could let you pick a favorite AI model in iOS 27
item.description: The next update to Apple's operating systems could allow users to choose their preferred AI model for running Apple Intelligence. According to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, Apple is planning to allow...

item.title: Threads is finally getting DMs on the web
item.description: Nearly a year after rolling out Threads direct messaging to everyone on mobile, Meta is finally bringing the feature to the web. Meta says it's "testing" DMs on the web now, but plans to make the...

item.title: Pennsylvania sues Character.AI after a chatbot allegedly posed as a doctor
item.description: According to Pennsylvania's filing, a Character.AI chatbot presented itself as a licensed psychiatrist during a state investigation, and also fabricated a serial number for its state medical license.

item.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of...

item.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 Instant, a new default model for ChatGPT
item.description: The company said the model reduces hallucination in sensitive areas such as law, medicine, and finance, while maintaining the low latency of its predecessor.

item.title: OpenAI claims ChatGPT’s new default model hallucinates way less
item.description: OpenAI's newest default model for ChatGPT might not make stuff up as much. Hallucinations have been an ongoing problem for AI models, but OpenAI says its new GPT-5.5 Instant model has "significant...

item.title: Book publishers sue Meta over AI’s ‘word-for-word’ copying
item.description: Meta is facing a class action lawsuit filed by five major book publishers and one author over claims the company "engaged in one of the most massive infringements of copyrighted materials in...

item.title: Microsoft’s new Xbox shake-up is all about platform changes
item.description: Microsoft's new Xbox chief, Asha Sharma, has spent the past couple of months making her mark on the Xbox organization. After focusing on highly requested Xbox console features, reducing the price...

item.title: Critical Apache HTTP/2 Flaw (CVE-2026-23918) Enables DoS and Potential RCE
item.description: The Apache Software Foundation (ASF) has released security updates to address several security vulnerabilities in the HTTP Server, including a severe vulnerability that could potentially lead to...

item.title: DAEMON Tools Supply Chain Attack Compromises Official Installers with Malware
item.description: A newly identified supply chain attack targeting DAEMON Tools software has compromised its installers to serve a malicious payload, according to findings from Kaspersky. "These installers are...

item.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
item.description: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
